UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's core profile information and references to their personalized settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

role.description: The user's role within the application (e.g., 'standard', 'admin').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

lastSeen.description: Timestamp of the user's last activity.

defaultUserPreferenceId.description: Reference to the UserPreference entity representing the user's default preferences. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 UserPreference)

defaultUserLayoutId.description: Reference to the UserLayout entity representing the user's default dashboard layout. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 UserLayout)

defaultModeId.description: Identifier for the default mode the user is set to experience (e.g., 'MinimalMode', 'FocusMode').

UserPreference.title: UserPreference
UserPreference.description: Stores user-specific settings and preferences, linked to a specific user and potentially a specific mode.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPreference entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity this preference belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserPreference)

schemaVersion.description: Version of the schema used for this preference configuration, for migration purposes.

lastMigratedAt.description: Timestamp when this preference set was last migrated to a newer schema version.

visualProfileId.description: Reference to the VisualProfile entity defining visual settings for this preference. (Relationship: UserPreference 1:1 VisualProfile)

modeId.description: Identifier of the application mode to which these preferences apply (e.g., 'MinimalMode').

VisualProfile.title: VisualProfile
VisualProfile.description: Defines a set of visual settings that can be applied to user preferences or layouts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VisualProfile entity.

name.description: A user-friendly name for this visual profile (e.g., 'Default Calm Profile').

motion.description: Controls the level of UI animations and motion (e.g., 'reduced', 'full').

fontScaling.description: Controls the scaling of fonts in the UI (e.g., 'small', 'medium', 'large').

UserLayout.title: UserLayout
UserLayout.description: Stores a user's specific dashboard layout configuration for a given mode.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLayout entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity this layout belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserLayout)

schemaVersion.description: Version of the schema used for this layout configuration, for migration purposes.

modeId.description: Identifier of the application mode to which this layout applies (e.g., 'ProDeskMode').

layoutName.description: A user-friendly name for this specific layout (e.g., 'My Market Watch').

gridConfiguration.description: JSON string representing the grid structure and arrangement of panels within the layout.

panelConfigurationIds.description: References to PanelConfiguration entities included in this layout. (Relationship: UserLayout N:N PanelConfiguration)

PanelConfiguration.title: PanelConfiguration
PanelConfiguration.description: Defines the specific configuration for a single data panel within a dashboard layout.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PanelConfiguration entity.

panelState.description: JSON string representing the specific state and properties of the panel (e.g., chart symbol, time range, news filters).

stableId.description: A stable identifier for the panel configuration instance, as per app requirements for schema versioning.

ExperimentAssignment.title: ExperimentAssignment
ExperimentAssignment.description: Records a user's assignment to a specific variant within an A/B test or experiment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExperimentAssignment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that is part of this experiment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ExperimentAssignment)

experimentName.description: The name of the A/B test or experiment (e.g., 'onboarding_variant').

variant.description: The specific variant assigned to the user (e.g., 'workflow', 'brain_UI').

assignedAt.description: Timestamp when the user was assigned to this experiment variant.

FeedbackItem.title: FeedbackItem
FeedbackItem.description: Stores user-submitted feedback, bug reports, or 'confusion points'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FeedbackItem entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who submitted the feedback. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FeedbackItem)

description.description: Detailed description of the feedback, issue, or confusion.

screenshotUrl.description: Optional URL to a screenshot or image supporting the feedback.

status.description: Current status of the feedback item (e.g., 'new', 'in_review', 'resolved', 'closed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the feedback item was submitted.

resolvedAt.description: Timestamp when the feedback item was resolved, if applicable.

UserEvent.title: UserEvent
UserEvent.description: Logs significant user actions and events for analytics and behavioral insights.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserEvent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity associated with this event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserEvent)

eventData.description: Optional JSON string containing additional context or properties for the event (e.g., {'modeId': 'MinimalMode'}).

AIChatThread.title: AIChatThread
AIChatThread.description: Represents a conversational thread with the AI assistant, storing metadata about the conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIChatThread entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who initiated this chat thread. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AIChatThread)

title.description: An auto-generated or user-provided summary/title for the chat thread.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the chat thread was created.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last message or update within the chat thread.

AIChatMessage.title: AIChatMessage
AIChatMessage.description: Represents a single message within an AI chat thread, including content and role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIChatMessage entity.

chatThreadId.description: Reference to the AIChatThread entity this message belongs to. (Relationship: AIChatThread 1:N AIChatMessage)

role.description: The role of the message sender (e.g., 'user', 'assistant', 'tool').

content.description: The full message content, potentially including JSON for tool calls or thought signatures as returned by the AI model.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent or received.

definition.description: Stores core profile information for each user. Access is restricted to the owning user. This document implicitly includes the user's role, but for critical authorization, the '/roles_admin/{userId}' collection should be used.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Contains user-specific preferences, organized under the user's profile. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `userId` is denormalized via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, inherited from the parent path.

params.name: preferenceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user preference set (e.g., 'main', or a mode-specific ID).

definition.description: Stores user-specific visual settings or customizations. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `userId` is denormalized via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, inherited from the parent path.

params.name: visualProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific visual profile.

definition.description: Manages individual dashboard layouts configured by the user. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `userId` is denormalized via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, inherited from the parent path.

params.name: layoutId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user layout.

definition.description: Defines the specific configuration for a single panel within a user's dashboard layout. Access is restricted to the owning user via the hierarchical path. The `userId` and `layoutId` are denormalized via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, inherited from the grandparent path.

params.name: layoutId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent user layout, inherited from the parent path.

params.name: panelConfigurationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific panel configuration.

definition.description: Records a user's assignment to A/B test variants. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `userId` is denormalized via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, inherited from the parent path.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for an experiment assignment.

definition.description: Logs significant user actions and events for analytics. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `userId` is denormalized via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, inherited from the parent path.

params.name: userEventId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user event.

definition.description: Represents a conversational thread with the AI assistant, owned by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `userId` is denormalized via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, inherited from the parent path.

params.name: chatThreadId
params.description: The unique identifier for an AI chat thread.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within an AI chat thread. To ensure Authorization Independence, this document includes a denormalized 'userId' field in addition to being nested under the user's path. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, inherited from the grandparent path and denormalized into the document.

params.name: chatThreadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent AI chat thread, inherited from the parent path.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chat message.

definition.description: Stores user-submitted feedback items. Each document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for Authorization Independence, enabling users to create and read their own feedback, and administrators to list all feedback. This supports Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs).

params.name: feedbackItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a feedback item.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for managing administrative roles based on user ID. This collection follows the 'Existence over Content' principle for DBAC, allowing efficient and secure checks for admin privileges without relying on custom claims or detailed user profile reads. The document content can be minimal (e.g., an empty object or a timestamp).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who possesses the admin role.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) through a combination of path-based ownership and strategic denormalization. Most user-specific data, such as `UserProfile` itself, `UserPreference`, `UserLayout`, `VisualProfile` (when user-specific), `ExperimentAssignment`, `UserEvent`, and `AIChatThread`, are structured under hierarchical paths like `/users/{userId}/...`. This approach inherently denormalizes the `userId` into the document's path, allowing Firestore Security Rules to directly evaluate `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing any `get()` operations on parent documents. This ensures atomic authorization checks and simplifies rule logic considerably.

For entities like `AIChatMessage`, which are nested deeply (e.g., `/users/{userId}/aiChatThreads/{chatThreadId}/messages/{messageId}`), the `userId` will also be explicitly denormalized within the `AIChatMessage` document itself. This ensures that even for operations on a message, ownership can be verified atomically by checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`, rather than performing a `get()` on the parent `AIChatThread`.

For `FeedbackItem` entities, which are conceptually submitted by a user but may require listing by administrators, a top-level collection `/feedbackItems/{feedbackItemId}` is used. To maintain Authorization Independence and support QAPs, each `FeedbackItem` document includes the `userId` of the submitter. This allows for simple rules like `allow create: if request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId;` and secure admin `list` operations (e.g., `allow list: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid));`), while preventing regular users from listing all feedback items. Users can still read their own feedback by checking `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`.

Global roles for Database Access Control (DBAC) are implemented using an 'existence over content' strategy with a dedicated top-level collection: `/roles_admin/{userId}`. An administrator's presence in this collection signifies their role, allowing security rules to check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` for granting elevated permissions. This is more robust and scalable than storing roles within the `UserProfile` document for critical authorization.

Structural Segregation is applied by ensuring that all documents within a given collection path share the same security posture (e.g., all documents under `/users/{userId}/preferences` are owned by `userId`). This radically simplifies security rule writing and debugging. Predictable schemas, explicit state modeling (e.g., `status` field for `FeedbackItem`), and consistent naming conventions further enhance debuggability and maintainability.
